behavior_summary: Handles the item's 'U8P_MACE2::look' (examine) event: when an actor inspects the mace, the script prepares item/info and process-exclude state, then directs the item to 'bark' a short description string "mace " to the requesting process/actor.
The routine invokes Item::bark targeted at the passed-in process/actor, captures the bark return value, suspends/waits on the bark result, and returns that value to the caller.
No global flags are read or written by this script excerpt (no persistent world-state changes observed).
notable_control_flow: Entry on a trigger-style event (Event 0: U8P_MACE2::look) that runs in the item's process context.

A short call sequence that builds/pushes a literal string, calls Item::bark, frees the temporary string, performs an implies/pop style control operation, and issues a suspend/wait before returning the bark result.
The function's return value is the direct return from Item::bark (propagated back to the caller).
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of the 'set info' and 'process exclude' opcodes here — which object receives the info and whether the calling process or the item is excluded (inferred but not explicit).
Purpose of the zero-initialized local (init_flag) at start — appears to be a placeholder or boolean but its concrete use is not present in the excerpt.
Whether any side-effects beyond the bark (e.g., visual/audio cues or logging) occur as a result of Item::bark in the full engine implementation.

main_function.name: U8P_MACE2::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to an actor's 'U8P_MACE2::look' action on the mace by preparing item/info state and emitting a short description ('mace ') via Item::bark to the requesting process, then returning the bark result.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_MACE2::look) — triggered when an actor inspects or 'looks' at the mace item

function_map.name: U8P_MACE2::look
function_map.role: Handle the mace item's 'U8P_MACE2::look' event and produce a short descriptive bark to the requesting process.

external_calls.purpose: Request that the item/process emit/display the short description string "mace " to the given target process/actor; return value is captured and returned by MACE2::look.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Handles the item's 'U8P_MACE2::look' (examine) event: when an actor inspects the mace, the script prepares item/info and process-exclude state, then directs the item to 'bark' a short description string "mace " to the requesting process/actor.
